name: Thumbprints/Current Jelly
recipeInstructions:
DESCRIPTION: Jelly jewels in a butter cookie nest dusted with nuts.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Mix together butter, sugar, egg yolk, and vanilla until well combined. Gradually incorporate flour and salt until the dough holds together. Form the dough into 1-inch balls and place them about 2 inches apart on an ungreased baking sheet. Lightly beat the egg white and dip each ball first in the beaten egg white, then roll it in walnuts. Press your thumb gently into the center of each cookie to create a hole.
Bake for 10 minutes or until lightly browned. Remove from the oven immediately and fill each indentation with jelly. Fill while the cookies are still warm to ensure the jelly melts evenly. Note: Use Red or Black Current Jelly for filling. These cookies freeze well if separated by layers of wax paper (to prevent the jelly from spreading) and stored in airtight containers.
Note: For optimal results, fill the cookies while they are still warm, as this helps the jelly melt evenly. The cookies can also be frozen after baking but should be stored with layers of wax paper between each cookie to prevent the jelly from spreading.
